perturbation.original_text: MA shall execute a Company License Agreement/Client Registration Form with Clients and present that License Agreement/Client Registration Form to Company after each purchase of the Technology. Upon the execution of this Agreement, Company shall provide copies of its end user license agreements to MA. MA shall promptly review such agreements and advise Company as to what revisions, if any, should be made to the end user license agreements for resale in the Territory set forth in Schedule A to ensure that the agreements comply with requirements of local law in the Territory, and that Company has protection concerning proprietary rights, warranty disclaimers and limitations of liability under such local and federal laws of the U.S. For purposes of this Agreement, the Technology means the electronic access to programs, content and documentation, and Company's end user license agreement as it may be modified by Company for use in the Territory. The relationship between the corporate user and Company and/or its Affiliates shall be as specified in the applicable Company end user license agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: MA shall execute a client registration form with Clients and present that client registration form to Company after each purchase of the Technology. Upon the execution of this Agreement, MA shall review such agreements and advise Company as to what revisions, if any, should be made to the agreements for resale in the Territory set forth in Schedule A to ensure that the agreements comply with requirements of local law in the Territory, and that Company has protection concerning proprietary rights, warranty disclaimers and limitations of liability under such local and federal laws of the U.S. For purposes of this Agreement, the Technology means the electronic access to programs, content and documentation. The relationship between the corporate user and Company and/or its Affiliates shall be at MA's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'Company License Agreement', the agreement is no longer tied to a specific legal document. Also removing the phrase 'Company's end user license agreement as it may be modified by Company for use in the Territory' creates ambiguity regarding what agreement governs the Technology's use. The phrase 'the relationship between the corporate user and Company and/or its Affiliates shall be at MA's discretion' is in direct contradiction with the rest of the agreement, particularly Section 3.4, as the intention of this agreement is to define the relationship between all parties involved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, as between Company and MA, MA shall be responsible as defined for providing customer and technical support to end users in the Territory. MA will notify Company immediately in the event that it is unable to respond effectively to any end users' requests.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'Company License Agreement' and modifies the definition of 'Technology', creating ambiguity about the governing agreement. More crucially, the changed text states 'The relationship between the corporate user and Company and/or its Affiliates shall be at MA's discretion', which directly contradicts the contradicted text that specifies 'MA shall be responsible as defined for providing customer and technical support to end users in the Territory,' thus dictating responsibilities and limiting MA's discretion, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Sales and Technical Training . MA shall attend initial marketing, application, and technical training as required and provided by Company; provided, however, that Company shall not require MA to market or support Technology according to a marketing plan or system prescribed in substantial part by Company. The MA will however, be responsible to display and explain in detail the methods by which they plan to achieve the assigned quotas. Sales Reports. MA shall report periodically in writing, upon request to Company the status of the following: Leads provided by Company Marketing activities in progress Sales forecasts Implementations in progress MA shall appoint one of its employees to be responsible for such reporting and make the name of such employee available to Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Sales and Technical Training . MA shall attend initial marketing, application, and technical training as required; provided, however, that Company shall not require MA to market or support Technology. Sales Reports. MA shall report periodically in writing, upon request to Company the status of the following: Leads provided by Company Marketing activities in progress Implementations in progress MA shall appoint one of its employees to be responsible for such reporting and make the name of such employee available to Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'provided by Company' after 'as required', as well as the phrase 'The MA will however, be responsible to display and explain in detail the methods by which they plan to achieve the assigned quotas' and the phrase 'Sales Forecasts' from the Sales Reports section, introduces an in-text contradiction with the section 3.11, due to the annual quotas being in place, and the fact that they need to report the status of the leads, marketing activities and implementations, yet the sales forecasts are omitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MA shall submit an annual marketing plan to Company outlining, among other things, activities and staffing directed at attaining mutually agreed upon annual sales quotas. The Annual Quota is defined in Schedule A of this Agreement. The annual marketing plan shall be devised solely by MA and MA shall not be required to follow an operating plan, standard procedure, training manual, or its substantial equivalent, published by Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by removing the requirement for the MA to explain their methods for achieving assigned quotas and removing 'Sales Forecasts' from the Sales Reports section, contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text for the MA to submit an annual marketing plan aimed at attaining 'mutually agreed upon annual sales quotas'. Without sales forecasts, it's unclear how the MA can create a meaningful annual marketing plan or how the Company can assess progress towards the quotas, creating a direct conflict and unenforceability issues.

perturbation.original_text: Company reserves the right to change its process and/or fees, from time to time, in its sole and absolute discretion. In the event of a price and/or fee change for Technology, Company shall notify MA in writing ninety (90) days prior to the effective date of any such change. All other prices and/or fees may be adjusted without prior notice to MA.
perturbation.changed_text: Company reserves the right to change its process and/or fees, from time to time, in its sole and absolute discretion. In the event of a price and/or fee change for Technology, Company shall notify MA in writing ninety (90) days prior to the effective date of any such change.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence, 'All other prices and/or fees may be adjusted without prior notice to MA', creates an in-text contradiction with section 4.2. The original section 4.2.(a) states that the prices shown in schedule A are subject to change 'pursuant to paragraph 13 of this Agreement'. This implies that schedule A, which contains more than just prices for the technology, cannot have it's other factors adjusted without prior notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Upon the request of MA, Company shall at prices or fees then in effect or mutually agreed upon by the parties:

Provide Technology to MA at the discounted prices shown in Schedule A, subject to Company's right to change its prices or fees pursuant to paragraph 13 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that 'All other prices and/or fees may be adjusted without prior notice to MA.' The changed text removes this statement. Section 4.2.(a) states that the prices in Schedule A are subject to change 'pursuant to paragraph 13 of this Agreement.' By removing the sentence about other price adjustments, the changed text implicitly contradicts section 4.2.(a) by creating the expectation that all prices in Schedule A now require 90 days' notice, whereas previously, only the technology prices required this notice. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original text provides an exception to the 90-day rule that the changed text has removed.
